scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, verified against the real published OpenAPI 3.0 spec (docs/api-reference/v2/openapi.json in github.com/calcom/cal.com, 121 method+path endpoints across 82 paths). 5 read streams (bookings, event_types, schedules, webhooks, my_profile) and 13 writes (booking lifecycle, event-type CRUD, schedule CRUD, webhook create/delete) are now covered — every other endpoint is either platform/OAuth-client management (Cal.com's white-label reseller surface, not tenant data), calendar-provider account wiring, a non-data verification/action endpoint, a binary/large-payload endpoint, or a narrower duplicate of an already-covered resource. The prior draft's ENGINE_GAP on event_types (a supposed data.eventTypeGroups[].eventTypes[] two-level nesting) was DISPROVEN by this research: that nested envelope belongs to an older, non-default API version; the documented current version (cal-api-version: 2024-06-14, required per the OpenAPI spec) returns a flat {status, data: EventTypeOutput[]} envelope identical in shape to every other list stream in this bundle, fully expressible via a plain records.path: "data" stream -- no engine gap exists. Live-verified 2026-07-04 (see spec.json's api_version description) that a single shared cal-api-version: 2024-06-14 header correctly routes bookings/schedules/event-types/webhooks/me simultaneously, despite each resource family separately documenting a distinct 'required' version value in the spec.

excluded.reason: single-booking detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the bookings list stream (GET /v2/bookings), which supports server-side filters (bookingUid, status, date ranges) for any targeted lookup a consumer would otherwise use this for
seat-scoped alias of the single-booking detail fetch above; same reasoning

attendee sub-collection is already embedded in each bookings-stream record's attendees[] array (BookingOutput_2024_08_13.attendees); a separate per-booking fan-out stream would duplicate that data, not add new coverage
adds a single attendee to an existing booking; a narrow compound-write variant of reschedule/create not modeled separately in this wave
single-attendee detail fetch; same data already present in the bookings stream's embedded attendees[] array
removes an attendee from a booking outside the normal cancel/reschedule lifecycle; narrow destructive action, not modeled in this wave
returns add-to-calendar deep links (Google/Outlook/ICS URLs) for a booking, a UI convenience, not syncable object data
video-conferencing session metadata requires a connected conferencing app (Zoom/Google Meet) OAuth grant beyond the api_key credential this bundle configures
adds guest emails to an existing booking; narrow compound-write variant not modeled in this wave
updates only the location field of a booking; narrower than reschedule/cancel, not modeled as its own write in this wave
marks host/attendee no-show on a booking; narrow status-flag write not modeled in this wave
round-robin host reassignment requires team/organization-scoped host-pool configuration beyond a single api_key credential's natural scope
explicit-user reassignment variant of the above; same reasoning
returns Cal Video recording download links/binary media metadata, not tabular sync data
internal calendar/video-provider booking references (external system correlation ids), operational metadata rather than a syncable business object
returns Cal Video meeting transcript download links, not tabular sync data
single-event-type detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the event_types list stream
creates a single-use/hidden private booking link for an event type; narrow sub-resource write not modeled in this wave
lists private links for a single event type; would require a fan_out over the event_types stream for marginal additional data not otherwise needed
updates a single private link; narrow sub-resource write, see above
deletes a single private link; narrow sub-resource write, see above

event-type-scoped webhook creation; functionally the same resource as the account-level create_webhook write (CreateWebhookInputDto shape), scoped narrower to one event type rather than the whole account
event-type-scoped webhook list; the webhooks stream (GET /v2/webhooks) already covers the account-level webhook resource
bulk-deletes every webhook on an event type in one call; broader blast radius than the single-webhook delete_webhook write
event-type-scoped webhook update; narrower variant of the account-level webhook resource, not modeled separately
event-type-scoped single-webhook detail fetch; see above
event-type-scoped webhook delete; the account-level delete_webhook write already covers deleting a webhook by id
returns the authenticated user's default schedule, a filtered view of the schedules list stream (isDefault: true); no data not already covered
single-schedule detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the schedules list stream
single-webhook detail fetch; every field it returns is already emitted by the webhooks list stream
updates the authenticated user's own profile (email changes require a separate verification flow); a single-tenant self-service action out of scope for a data-integration connector in this wave
in-place webhook update (change url/triggers/active); create_webhook + delete_webhook cover the common lifecycle, an update action deferred to a later wave
rotates/invalidates the caller's own API key, breaking every other in-flight integration using it; never a safe automated action
OAuth client (platform/reseller) metadata, not tenant scheduling data; requires platform-level credentials distinct from a per-account api_key
OAuth2 token-exchange endpoint; a credential/auth-flow primitive, not a syncable data resource
OAuth token refresh primitive; same reasoning
lists the user's connected external calendar-provider accounts (Google/Outlook); requires those providers' own OAuth grants, not this connector's api_key credential
reads busy/free time from a connected external calendar; same credential-scope reasoning
connected-calendar-account listing; same reasoning
reads events from a connected external calendar; same reasoning
creates an event on a connected external calendar; same reasoning
single external-calendar-event detail fetch; same reasoning
updates an event on a connected external calendar; same reasoning
deletes an event on a connected external calendar; same reasoning
free/busy read from a connected external calendar; same reasoning
validates an ICS calendar feed subscription; external-calendar account wiring, not tenant scheduling data
saves an ICS calendar feed subscription; same reasoning
validates a specific calendar-provider connection; same reasoning
starts an OAuth connect flow for a calendar provider; an interactive auth-flow endpoint, not a data resource
stores calendar-provider OAuth credentials; account-wiring, not tenant data
disconnects a connected calendar-provider account, breaking any scheduling that depends on it
single external-calendar-event detail (singular-path variant); same reasoning as the plural /events/{eventUid} entries
updates a single external-calendar event (singular-path variant); same reasoning
lists events on one calendar-provider connection; same reasoning
creates an event on one calendar-provider connection; same reasoning
single external-calendar-event detail; same reasoning
updates a single external-calendar event; same reasoning
deletes a single external-calendar event; same reasoning
free/busy read for one calendar-provider connection; same reasoning
completes an OAuth connect callback for a calendar provider; interactive auth-flow endpoint, not a data resource
sets which connected external calendar new bookings are written to; depends on a calendar-provider OAuth grant this connector does not hold
selects which connected calendars count toward availability; same calendar-provider-credential reasoning
deselects a connected calendar; same reasoning
lists connected video-conferencing apps (Zoom/Google Meet); requires those providers' own OAuth grants
default conferencing app selection; same reasoning
connects a conferencing app account; interactive auth-flow, same reasoning
sets the default conferencing app; same reasoning
disconnects a connected conferencing app, breaking any event type relying on it for meeting links
returns an OAuth authorization URL for a conferencing app; interactive auth-flow primitive
OAuth callback handler for a conferencing app connect flow; not a data resource
creates a platform (white-label reseller) OAuth client; a platform-admin capability, not tenant scheduling data, requiring platform credentials distinct from a per-account api_key
lists platform OAuth clients; same reasoning
single OAuth client detail; same reasoning
updates a platform OAuth client; same reasoning
deletes a platform OAuth client; same reasoning
lists a platform OAuth client's managed users; platform-admin scope
creates a managed user under a platform OAuth client; same reasoning
single managed-user detail; same reasoning
updates a managed user; same reasoning
deletes a managed user; same reasoning
forces a managed user's token refresh; platform-admin scope
creates a webhook scoped to a platform OAuth client; platform-admin scope, not the account-level webhooks resource
lists OAuth-client-scoped webhooks; same reasoning
bulk-deletes OAuth-client-scoped webhooks; same reasoning
updates an OAuth-client-scoped webhook; same reasoning
single OAuth-client-scoped webhook detail; same reasoning
deletes an OAuth-client-scoped webhook; same reasoning
computes available booking time slots for an event type at query time (a derived availability calculation, not a persisted, syncable record set)
temporarily holds a slot before booking; an ephemeral reservation-lock primitive, not a durable data write
reads an ephemeral slot-reservation's remaining hold time; not a durable data resource
extends an ephemeral slot reservation; same reasoning as the create endpoint above
releases an ephemeral slot reservation; same reasoning
checks Stripe payment-integration connection status; requires a separate Stripe OAuth grant, not tenant scheduling data
starts the Stripe Connect OAuth flow; interactive auth-flow, same reasoning
completes the Stripe Connect OAuth callback; same reasoning
lists the user's verified secondary email addresses; account-settings surface, not scheduling data
sends a verification code; an auth/notification action, not a data write
verifies a code; same reasoning
single verified-email detail; same account-settings-surface reasoning
lists verified phone numbers; same reasoning
sends an SMS verification code; auth/notification action
verifies a code; same reasoning
single verified-phone detail; same account-settings-surface reasoning
